corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: token is a nominal plural contribution such as 'the successful ones'; exact token review required before live repair
what_failed: A populated hover inherited the Qamus verb/root infinitive even though the Qur'anic token is a nominal active-participle plural.
sarf_lesson: A nominal derivative must keep its gloss shape. Do not accept a 'to ...' infinitive on an ism fāʿil plural merely because the root meaning is familiar.
nahw_lesson: After sarf fixes the nominal shape, nahw may explain the predicate/subject role; iʿrāb cannot rescue a wrong verb-shaped hover.
learner_explanation: ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ names the successful ones; it is not the finite action 'to succeed'.
drill_prompt: Compare أَفْلَحَ and ٱلْمُفْلِحُونَ. Which one can be glossed with 'to', and which names people?

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: token is a noun/result such as 'a structure/building/canopy'; exact token review required before live repair
what_failed: The visible hover used a verb infinitive on a nominal result token.
sarf_lesson: A nominal/result form from a verbal root is not the finite verb. Shape the hover as a noun before any semantic polishing.
nahw_lesson: Case can place the noun in the sentence, but it cannot turn a noun/result into the verb 'to build'.
learner_explanation: بِنَآءً points to a built thing/structure, not the action 'to build'.
drill_prompt: Given بِنَآءً, reject 'to build' and write a noun-shaped hover.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: token is a passive/adjectival contribution such as 'purified'; exact token review required before live repair
what_failed: The visible hover used a transitive verb infinitive where the surface is a passive participle/adjectival token.
sarf_lesson: Read the participle pattern and voice before glossing; a passive participle contributes a state/patient meaning, not the causative verb.
nahw_lesson: Nahw can locate the adjective in the phrase, but the English shape must already preserve the passive participle contribution.
learner_explanation: مُّطَهَّرَةٌ describes something purified; it does not mean 'to purify'.
drill_prompt: Mark مُّطَهَّرَةٌ as a passive/adjectival form and rewrite the hover without 'to'.

corrected_hover_or_pending_reason: pending: token is an active participle contribution such as 'one who makes/places'; exact token review required before live repair
what_failed: The visible hover copied the entry/root verb shape onto an active participle token.
sarf_lesson: Ism fāʿil names the doer/agent. It cannot be rich-certified with a bare infinitive gloss.
nahw_lesson: Context may decide whether 'make' or 'place' is better, but nahw must work after the active-participle shape is preserved.
learner_explanation: جَاعِلٌ means one who makes or places; it is not the command or infinitive 'to make'.
drill_prompt: Rewrite جَاعِلٌ as an active participle and state why family-wide propagation is unsafe without context.
